prompt:
The team wants a daily word game on the shared dev box, Wordle rules, but with our own word lists checked into the repo so game N is the same for everyone. One file, wordle.py, stdlib only.

Word lists: answers.txt is the ordered list of daily answers; allowed.txt is the extra guessable vocabulary. Both are one lowercase 5-letter word per line (skip blank lines when loading). A guess is legal if it's in EITHER file — the answers are always guessable too.

Module API our stats scripts import: `score_guess(guess, answer)` returns a 5-char string of 'G' (right letter, right spot), 'Y' (right letter, wrong spot), '-' (not in the word). Get the duplicate-letter rule exactly right, it's the whole point: first, all greens are decided and each green consumes that letter from the answer; then the remaining positions are judged LEFT TO RIGHT, each 'Y' consuming one leftover copy of its letter — when the copies run out, later duplicates score '-'. Mismatched lengths raise ValueError. `hard_mode_error(prev_guess, prev_feedback, guess)` returns None if the guess honours the previous round's feedback, else a refusal string: every green position must be replayed in place — check positions left to right and report the first offence as 'hard mode: letter N must be X' (N is 1-based, X uppercase) — and after that every letter marked G or Y must appear in the guess at least as many times as it was marked; check letters alphabetically and report the first shortfall as 'hard mode: not enough X'.

CLI: `python3 wordle.py ANSWERS ALLOWED GAME [--hard]` where GAME is the 1-based line number of the answer. Read guesses from stdin, one per line, case-insensitive (lowercase them). Anything that isn't a legal 5-letter word from the lists prints 'not a valid word' and does NOT use up a turn. With --hard, a guess that violates the previous accepted guess's feedback prints the refusal message and also costs nothing. An accepted guess prints its feedback string; guessing the answer then prints 'solved in K' (K = accepted guesses so far) and exits 0; a 6th accepted guess that still isn't the answer prints 'out of guesses: ANSWER' and exits 0. If stdin ends mid-game, just exit 0.

The acceptance contract is test_wordle.py with the two word-list fixtures — python3 test_wordle.py must pass, tests and fixtures stay as they are.
